children.name: Caption
children.characters: Estética y fuerza en básicos + variantes Zercher

1:1549.name: Configuration=Small, Elevation=Flat
1:1549.description:
The top app bar provides content and actions related to the current screen. It’s used for branding, screen titles, navigation, and actions.


[GDP / GM3 Design System] 🔍

1:171.name: Type=Round, Size=Small, Width=Narrow, State=Enabled
1:171.description:
Standard Icon buttons are low-emphasis buttons.
They are used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options.

Icon buttons can be placed on a variety of backgrounds. Until the button is interacted with, its container isn’t visible.

1:841.name: Type=Round, Size=Small, Width=Default, State=Enabled
1:841.description:
Standard Icon buttons are low-emphasis buttons.
They are used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options.

Icon buttons can be placed on a variety of backgrounds. Until the button is interacted with, its container isn’t visible.

1:1728.name: Style=Elevated, Configuration=Label & icon, State=Enabled
1:1728.description: Enabled elevated assistive chip with label & icon

1:1545.name: App bar
1:1545.description:
Use a top app bar to provide content and actions related to a current screen, such as navigation, screen headlines, and actions.


Focus on describing the current page and provide 1–2 essential actions.
Displays labels and page navigation controls at the top of the page. (Use a toolbar to display page actions)
Three types: small, medium flexible, large flexible
On scroll, apply a fill color to separate from body content
Can animate on and off screen with another bar of controls, like a row of chips


Guidelines

1:1683.name: Assistive chip
1:1683.description: Assist chips represent smart or automated actions that can span multiple apps. They should appear dynamically and contextually in a UI.

1:3411.name: Segmented button
1:3411.description:
Segmented buttons help people select options, switch views or sort elements. 

🛠 Additional options
Button segment - edit the states, style and icon options of button segments by selecting the individual segment and editing the variant properties.

1:3033.name: Building Blocks/Segmented button/Button segment (start)
1:3033.description: Segment button used at the start of segment button groups

1:3285.name: Building Blocks/Segmented button/Button segment (end)
1:3285.description: Segment button used at the end of segment button groups
